summary: Postinstall dropper fetches and executes remote code from hardcoded IP C2
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and performs a complete install-time RCE dropper sequence. It collects host fingerprint data (os.hostname(), os.userInfo().username, os.platform(), os.arch()), base64-encodes it as JSON, and sends it as a query parameter to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 endpoint at http://213.218.160.189:8080/s (with fallback to port 80) over plain HTTP. It then receives a code payload (optionally XOR-decrypted with a 32-byte hex key prefix), writes it to a randomly-named hidden.js file under /tmp (or %TEMP% on Windows), and spawns a detached, stdio-ignored, windowsHide node process to execute it. The staged file is unlinked ~5 seconds after spawn to remove forensic traces. The dropper also includes anti-analysis logic that aborts if Windows process names contain 'wireshark', 'fiddler', 'procmon', 'x64dbg', or 'ida'. This is textbook install-time remote code execution: bare-IP plain-HTTP fetch of opaque code, executed as a detached background process during npm install, with host fingerprinting and anti-debug evasion.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
